title: Onnu Rendu Lyrics Dharma Durai(1991)

ah neram vandhaachu

ippo
kenjum ennai minjalaama

enrichedMetadata.faq:
Frequently Asked Questions

What is the meaning of the song Onnu Rendu?

The song conveys themes of love and longing, reflecting on the beauty of relationships.

What is the context of this song in the movie Dharma Durai?

This song plays during a pivotal moment, highlighting the emotional connection between the characters.

Who are the singers of Onnu Rendu?

The song features the melodious voices of Mano and S Janaki.

When was Onnu Rendu released?

The song was released on January 14, 1991.
enrichedMetadata.summary: In Onnu Rendu, the singers Mano and S Janaki beautifully express the emotions of love and nostalgia. The song's lyrics reflect a longing for connection and the memories associated with it. Set against the backdrop of Dharma Durai, this melody resonates with anyone who has experienced the bittersweet nature of love. Its soothing tune and heartfelt delivery make it a cherished classic in Tamil cinema.
enrichedMetadata.high_ctr_intro: Onnu Rendu from Dharma Durai is a timeless melody that captures the essence of love and longing. With the enchanting voices of Mano and S Janaki, this song evokes a deep sense of nostalgia. Its heartfelt lyrics and soothing tune make it a perfect addition to any romantic playlist.
stanzaMeanings: One, two, three, four, five, six, which town is my own town, what is my name?
Ah, the time has come, the evening has arrived, will you come to me like a shadow for a while?
One, two, three, four, five, six, which town is my own town, what is my name?
Like a flower searching for fragrance, come to me, oh beautiful one.
In the boat, come and see the joy, you run and come.
Singing the divine songs, seeking happiness, come, my dear.
When the honey comes to the flower, come and dance.
Slowly come and share my wealth, tell me about that happiness.
When there is desire, the goddess does not let go.
Now will you come to me like a shadow?
One, two, three, four, five, six, which town is my own town, what is my name?
That time has come, the evening has arrived, will you come to me like a shadow for a while?
One, two, three, four, five, six, which town is my own town, what is my name?
Looking around, don’t sell a flower that cannot bloom.
The desire has become a madness, the mind has gone crazy, the wisdom has left.
With the wedding garland, come and express your desire.
You, the beautiful one, come and run to see.
Oh dear, showing your hair, everything is beautiful.
Will you give me half of your joy?
Oh maiden, you must come close, give love and live in heaven.
I will dance with you.
Now will you come to me like a shadow?
One, two, three, four, five, six, which town is my own town, what is my name?
The time has come, the evening has arrived, will you come to me like a shadow for a while? Now will you come to me like a shadow?
One, two, three, four, five, six, which town is my own town, what is my name?
